titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the William L. Holladay Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Michelle Peralta

conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Describing Archives: A Content Standard

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday February 27, 2020 at 23:25

langmaterial.language: Materials in the collection are primarily in English. German, Hebrew, Ancient Greek, Arabic, Aramaic, French, and Dutch are also represented in the collection.

did.abstract: The papers comprise the professional and personal correspondence, teaching files, writings, and public talks that document William L. Holladay's career as a scholar of the Old Testament, his teaching career at both the Near East School of Theology and the Andover Newton Theological School, and his work as a minister for the United Church of Christ.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The papers are arranged into four series: I. Correspondence, 1945-2008, II. Professional and teaching files, 1873-2012, III. Writings, speeches, and editorial work, 1946-2007, and IV. Personal papers, 1945-2002.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research with the exception of student grades in Series II. Professional and teaching files, which is restricted until January 1, 2060.
Access to original born-digitial materials is restricted until processed.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: William L. Holladay Papers (RG 302), Special Collections, Yale Divinity School Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The collection was arranged and described according to national and local standards. Processing staff have removed publications and duplicate materials from the collection.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright to materials authored or otherwise produced by William L. Holladay is retained by Patricia Applebaum. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: William L. Holladay was born June 23, 1926 in Dallas, Texas. He studied Greek and mathematics at the University of California at Berkeley before being drafted into the army in 1946 following World War II. In the army, he served as a chaplain's assistant until his discharge in 1947. Holladay returned to UC Berkeley and graduated summa cum laude in 1948.

Holladay then attended the Pacific School of Religion, studying with John Herbert Otwell and Georgia Harkness, and earning a bachelor of divinity degree in 1951. Holladay entered the Congregational ministry and was ordained in 1952, becoming the pastor of Murphys and Angels Camp until 1955.

Holladay attended the University of Leiden in the Netherlands where he studied under P. A. H. (Pieter Arie Hendrik) de Boer. His dissertation was titled "The Root subh in the Old Testament." After earning his doctorate in theology in 1958, Holladay and his family returned to the United States and spent two years in Colorado where Holladay served as the campus minister for the United Church of Christ (UCC) at the University of Colorado at Boulder. From 1960 to 1963, Holladay served on the faculty at Elmhurst College, teaching biblical studies and world religions, before accepting a position at the Near East School of Theology located in Beirut, Lebanon. Holladay was also commissioned as a missionary under the United Church Board for World Ministries. Occasionally, Holladay taught at the American University of Beirut. He was also involved in archaeology, working on a dig at Shechem with G. Earnest Wright in the summer of 1964 and leading archaeological tours for students to Jerusalem and Jordan in 1966 and 1967.

In 1970, Holladay and his family returned to the United States after he accepted a professorship as the Samuel Edgar Lowry Professor of Old Testament at the Andover Newton Theological School in Newton, Massachusetts. Holladay retired in 1997, but continued to publish, teach, and give public lectures. He died on May 6, 2016.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers comprise the professional and personal correspondence, teaching files, writings, and public talks that document Holladay's career as a scholar of the Old Testament, his teaching career at both the Near East School of Theology and the Andover Newton Theological School, and his work as a minister for the United Church of Christ. The papers include Holladay's study of ancient languages, including Hebrew, Aramaic, and Greek, as well as his interest in archaeology. Of particular note are the collected materials that provide a snapshot of the concerns of the American academic community living in the Middle East during the 1960s and the tensions between Israel and Palestine.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series is comprised of Holladay's incoming and outgoing correspondence documenting his research and teaching interests, collaborators, and work with professional associations. Correspondence pertaining to his publications is also included in the series. Additional correspondence may be found in Series II under topical files and professional organizations, as well as Series III. Writings, speeches, and editorial work.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in the series are arranged into two sub-series: by name or topic and by Holladay's publication title, and alphabetically therein.

did.unittitle: Andover Newton Theological School - "Jeremiah, Prophet for His Time and Ours"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence pertains to a series held at Andover Newton Theological School, as well as a Lenten program and sermon given at the United Congregational Church in Worchester, Massachusetts.

did.unittitle: Boer, Pieter Arie Hendrik (P. A. H.) de
Catholic Biblical Association of America - Catholic Biblical Quarterly

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence pertaining to Holladay's on-going election as an associate editor with the journal, and continued work as a reviewer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence primarily related to editorial decisions as members of the editorial board for Vetus Testamentum , a journal dedicated to Old Testament study.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence related to courses taught on Jeremiah and Isaiah.

did.unittitle: National Council of the Churches of Christ - Standard Bible Translation Committee

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence pertains primarily to the New Revised Standard Version (NRSV) of the Bible. Includes committee membership roster.

did.unittitle: Near East Ecumenical Bureau for Information-Interpretation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs.

did.unittitle: Near East School of Theology-Institute for Middle Eastern New Testament Studies
Oxford University Press - New Oxford Bible of the New Revised Standard Version
Seeking employment upon return to the United States, Regarding
Society of Biblical Literature - Composition of the book of Jeremiah

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, proposals, meeting notes, and papers for a consultation for the book of Jeremiah.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence pertains to churches in Massachusetts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains primarily correspondence with E. J. Brill and William B. Eerdmans Publishing Company regarding translation, reprints, and revision. Also includes publishing contracts and some of Holladay's notes on translating Koehler-Baumgartner's lexicon into English.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence pertains to the publication of A Concise Hebrew and Aramaic Lexicon of the Old Testament .

did.unittitle: Interpretation: A Journal of Bible and Theology - Permission to reprint

title.part: Isaiah, Scroll of a Prophetic Heritage

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains letters from potential publishers for the manuscript, as well as Holladay's notes on the galley proofs in preparation for publication.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence related to copyright.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence pertaining to Jeremiah 1 and Jeremiah 2 commentaries published in the "Hermeneia: A Critical and Historical Commentary on the Bible" series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence to and from Holladay and publisher, Fortress Press, regarding the printing of Jeremiah 1 . Also contains a proposal for the commentary.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence with Nahman Avigad of the Institute of Archaeology, Hebrew University of Jerusalem pertaining to a print of the bulla of Baruch for use in Jeremiah 2 's printing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence with publishers and authors for permission to reprint material.

did.unittitle: "Jeremiah in the Midst of Prophetic Words" - Institut für Bibelwissenschaft des Alten und Neuen Testamentes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence pertains to Holladay's article "Jeremiah in the Midst of Prophetic Words" to honor Franz Hubmann.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primarily contains correspondence with the Pilgrim Press, the United Church Press, William B. Eerdmans, although other publishers are represented. Also includes the publishing contract with the Pilgrim Press.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes publishing contract.

title.part: The Psalms Through Three Thousand Years: Prayerbook of a Cloud of Witnesses

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence with publisher, Augsburg Fortress, and publishing contract.

did.unittitle: "Songs for Christians: Using the Whole Psalter," Christian Century

title.part: Unbound by Time: Isaiah Still Speaks

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with publisher, Cowley Publications, publishing contract, and notes on lectionary selections on Isaiah.

title.part: What Are They Doing to Our Hymns?

did.unittitle: Series II: Professional and teaching files

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains materials related to Holladay's professional career as a professor and scholar. It also documents his work as a Congregational minister and missionary. The Teaching files sub-series chronicles his tenure as an Old Testament professor at the Near East School of Theology (Beirut, Lebanon) and the Andover Newton Theological School. Lecture notes and outlines, exams and quizzes, as well as grades comprise the types of materials in Holladay's Teaching files. Materials for workshops, seminars, and courses taught after his retirement are also represented in the sub-series.

Holladay's Topical files include materials on biblical studies and archaeology of the ancient Near East. The series also contains Holladay's collected materials on the Israeli-Palestinian conflict from his time in Lebanon, including newsletters, newspaper clippings, magazines and other international publications. Reprints and writings of scholars and activist groups, with a focus on refugees in Palestine, document the concerns, discussions, and experiences of the international community in the Middle East in the 1960s and 1970s.

Also included in the series are committee minutes, conference programs, and correspondence documenting Holladay's involvement in professional organizations, such as the Catholic Biblical Association of America and the Society of Biblical Literature, as well the editorial board of Vetus Testamentum .
Holladay's work as a Congregational minister, pastor, and missionary are documented in Ministerial files, which comprise sermons given and rites performed.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials are arranged into four sub-series: Teaching files, Topical files, Professional organizations, and Ministerial files.

did.unittitle: Near East School of Theology - Consultation on the Development and the Relocation of the Seminary
Scripture reading lists for doctoral students
Acts - Narrative of Church before Paul

scopecontent.p: Includes course notes and quizzes based on History of Israel by John Bright.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains lecture notes on Jeremiah for Joel Kaminsky's course on the prophets at Smith College.

Jesus - Sources for his life outside of the four gospels

Koinonia - Shared life of first generation Christians

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes materials on the Near East School of Theology.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Holladay's book review for Irony in the Old Testament written by Edwin M. Good.

God who acts; Names and characteristics of God; Monotheism
God and humankind; Names; Corporate personality and individualism

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes materials on festivals and marriage, sacrifice, sanctuaries, leadership, and magic, demonology, etc.

Time of oral traditions - Form criticism

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes sessions for a laymen's bible study.

did.unittitle: "Translating the Bible: Getting from There to Here" series

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials used in a series on Bible translation led by Holladay and Patricia Applebaum.

"Isaiah in Advent: Listening, Teaching, and Preaching"
"Is the Bible Here to Stay?: An Introduction to Scripture" series

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes two untitled sermons.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes lecture outlines for "New Light on Jeremiah: A Prophet in the Headlines of His Day" and "The Word from Jeremiah for Today."

"Long Ago God Spoke: How to Read the Bible" series

scopecontent.p: Includes notes for a series of lectures titled: "Old Testament and the Generation Gap"; "Old Testament and Revolutionary Change"; "Old Testament and the Individual Lost in the Crowd"; and "Old Testament and New Testament God: One God or Two?"

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Student grades are restricted until January 1, 2060.

scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Emily Gibbes from the National Council of Churches of Christ on the provided draft of "The Liberating Word: A Guide to Non-Sexist Interpretation of the Bible" by Letty M. Russell.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Holladay's translation of Hebrew text and vocabulary, as well as notes on usage.

langmaterial.language: Includes materials written in French, Dutch, German, and Arabic.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains maps, articles, notes and report from daughter Catherine Holladay.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence to Rabbi Marc Tannenbaum of the American Jewish Committee regarding the circulation of anti-Semitic material in Beiruit. Materials requested from the Anti-Defamation League of B'nai B'rith are also represented, as well as Holladay's "Remarks on the 'Protocoles of the Elders of Zion' by Anis Sayegh."

did.unittitle: Statements from churches in the Middle East

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains reports and memoranda pertaining to refugees in Palestine and Jordan by the United Nations and the World Council of Churches in the Middle East.

did.unittitle: Reports of deputations to the Middle East
United Nations General Assembly Resolutions Regarding Assistance to Palestine Refugees (1948-1967)
"Promise of Al-Assifah: A Collection of Poems from Occupied Territories"

Al-Fateh and the Palestinian Resistance Movement
Massachusetts Council of Churches - Middle East seminar
University Christian Center (UCC) Review - Middle East Crisis
"An Approach to Peace in the Middle East" (Quaker)
"Israel's Violation of Human Rights in the Occupied Territories: A Documented Report"

title.part: To Establish a Just and Lasting Peace: Selected United States Statements on the Middle East from 1967-1970

did.unittitle: Questions to the Jewish people in the State of Israel
World Conference of Christians, "Christian Faith and the Palestinian Problem"
World Conference of Christians for Palestine
Christians Committed to Justice and Reconciliation in Palestine
Abd-Al-Maseeh, Amos, "The Challenges and the Cross of the Church in the Arab World"
Davis, John, "Peace in the Holy Land?"
Nes, David, "The June War in the Middle East" plenary address

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Address given at the Conference on World Affairs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, reviser guidelines, and Old Testament Revision Committee minutes and policies. Also includes a draft for the book of Jeremiah revision as well as "Criteria for the Evaluation of Inclusive Language Translations of Scriptural Texts Proposed for Liturgical Use."

did.unittitle: Catholic Biblical Association of America meetings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains annual meeting notes, programs, correspondence, and membership rosters.

did.unittitle: International Organization for the Study of the Old Testament (IOSOT) congresses
Japan Bible Society - International Bible Forum

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes newspaper clippings, ephemera, proceedings, and correspondence related to the conference and Holladay's participation in the conference and his talk on Isaiah. Includes photograph of Holladay delivering talk, "How God Speaks to Christians Through Isaiah."

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to original born-digital materials are restricted until processed.

did.unittitle: National Council of the Churches of Christ - Revised Standard Bible Committee
Council on the Study of Religion bulletin
United Church of Christ - Stewardship Council

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: General materials in the section are arranged chronologically. Sermons are organized by topic or title are arranged alphabetically therein.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This sub-series documents Holladay's service as a Congregational minister. It includes his sermons delivered at various Christian celebrations and rites.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence pertaining to common law marriage and marriage by proxy.

did.unittitle: First Congressional Church of Murphys (California) - Murals

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs, newspaper clippings, and programs from the dedication of the murals. Murals

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence.

Near East School of Theology chapel sermons
Sermons, orders of worship, lectures - Netherlands
Andover Newton Theological School chapel sermons

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes and outlines for various sermons.

did.unittitle: "The Appearance of His Face Was Changed"
"Dreadful Portens and Great Signs from Heaven"
"Go Therefore and Make Disciples of All Nations"
"He Makes Those Who Are Bent Stand Straight!"
"He Was Despised and Rejected by Men"
"Those Who Say 'Yes,' and Those Who Say 'No'"
"Vision of Moses" and "Embodiment of the New Covenant"
"We Are Slaves, and We Are Free"
Series III: Writings, speeches, and editorial work

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series represents the work written or delivered by Holladay. Writings include articles, papers, book reviews and manuscripts created, both published and unpublished. Speeches include typescripts of public lectures, commencement speeches, and keynote addresses. Sermons are found in Ministerial files in Series II. Professional and teaching files. Holladay's work produced as a translator, and manuscript reviewer and reviser are documented in the Editorial work sub-series.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in the series are arranged into three sub-series: Writings, Speeches, and Editorial work, and alphabetically therein.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains notes and draft of paper, "The Aramaic Verse in Jeremiah Reconsidered" along with correspondence from D. C. Westermann and M. Dahood.

did.unittitle: "Bible Studies"- Fourteenth General Synod, United Church of Christ
"A Coherent Chronology of Jeremiah's Early Career"
"The Crux in Daniel 3:17 - A Proposed Solution"
"How Are Christians to Use the Psalms?"
"How the Twenty-third Psalm Became an American Secular Icon"
Living by the Word series, Christian Century
"The Misreading of 'Ālâ (curse) as 'Ēlleh (these) in the Texts of Jeremiah's Poetry"
"A Reply to Martin Stöhr: Israel and the New Left
"The Role of the Church Today," Advance

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains publisher correspondence, article drafts, and reference material.

did.unittitle: "Structure and Possible Setting of the New Covenant Passage, Jeremiah 31:31-34," In memoriam of Luis Alonso Schökel

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains reviewer notes, drafts, and correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains outline, reviewer notes, and book reviews. Also includes a passage from Holladay's unpublished memoir, detailing the creation of the manuscript.

Isaiah, Scroll of a Prophetic Heritage book reviews
Introduction: Literary history of the book of Jeremiah

scopecontent.p: Contains a draft of an introduction written for the "Hermeneia: A Critical and Historical Commentary on the Bible" series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials pertain to an early idea of a commentary on Jeremiah.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Holladay's remarks on the publication of Jeremiah 1 .

did.unittitle: Refinements to M. O'Connor's Colometry I and II

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material was eventually published in the Journal of Biblical Literature (1999) as two separate articles, "Hebrew Verse Structure Revisited (I): Which Words 'Count'?" (vol. 118, no. 1) and "Hebrew Verse Structure Revisited (II): Conjoint Cola, and Further Suggestions" (vol. 118, no.3).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains notes for a proposed revision of J. Weingreen's Hebrew grammar, which was declined by publishers.

title.part: The Psalms Through Three Thousand Years: Prayerbook of a Cloud of Witnesses

scopecontent.p: Includes hymnals, articles, notes, and manuscript outline.

scopecontent.p: Also includes correspondence in response to Holladay's commencement address.

scopecontent.p: Also includes correspondence with editors at Fortress Press.

did.unittitle: New American Bible - Book of Jeremiah
Hoftizjer, Jacob, "Prophet Balaam in a 6th-Century Aramaic Inscription"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Translated from Hoftijzer's address "De Onticijfering van Dier-Alla-Teksten" by Holladay and published in Biblical Archeologist .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains Holladay's personal and biographical materials, including photographs, a journal documenting his service during World War II, and his ordination and subsequent relinquishment of duties as a Congregational minister. Holladay's 1997 retirement celebration from the Andover Newton Theological School is documented by photographs and copies of speeches delivered. These files provide a broad overview of Holladay's life and career. His student work, particularly from the Pacific School fo Religion, is especially well documented in the series.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials are organized into two sub-series: General, and Student work. General materials are arranged chronologically while materials in Student work are arranged alphabetically by title or topic.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of small notebook detailing Holladay's military service in World War II.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs and copies of speeches delivered at the celebration marking Holladay's retirement from the Andover Newton Theological School. Also includes a letter conferring Holladay's emeritus status, and photographs and ephemera from Holladay's life and career used in a display.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains course work related to how to construct and write sermons.

langmaterial.language: Includes notes written in Hebrew, Syriac, and Arabic.
Includes notes written in Greek, Hebrew, and Arabic.
